Transcrição Completa: 81 Client Retention Strategies 8 10 24     [00:00] "All right. So, I think it was two weeks ago, we had a request from one of our inner circle members, Tom. Tom had specifically asked about client retention strategies because he was struggling a little bit with retention."   [00:15] "So, to be clear, doesn't matter whether you have a marketing agency, an info product business, a general business, retention is critical. Doesn't matter whether it's a transactional sale or a recurring sale. You really have to be able to master this in order to ensure first of all that you have a very low to literally zero refund rate, chargeback rate."   [00:35] "And to be clear, if you do have any kind of ongoing recurring charges coming into the business, all five things that we're about to talk about here today will ensure that you actually retain that revenue for as long as possible."   [00:48] "Just to put it in perspective, so you guys know I'm not full of shit - I retain clients for years on average. Our best deals literally stick with us quite literally for life. We have a really good consistent return on every deal that we do."   [01:05] "The deals that don't work, we actually proactively try to get them out of the business within about a month or two. At most, my bad deals stick around for about two months."   [01:15]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Client Retention Rate"   NÚMEROS:  92%   VISUAL:  Métrica de retenção destacada, demonstrando taxa de retenção de clientes da agência   [01:18] "Our retention rate on average right now, and this is a little crazy to be clear, but we sit around 92%. In terms of our average retention rate, meaning when we do take on a deal, we retain nine out of 10 people."   [01:32] "To be clear, this is true for all of our offers. But specifically, this is cumulative across anything that's a recurring service that my education company and my advertising agency offers."   [01:45]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "5-Point Client Retention Star"   NÚMEROS:  5 pontos   VISUAL:  Sistema estruturado em formato de estrela com cinco pontos principais para retenção de clientes   [01:50] "So, long story short, a few years ago, I wrote up an SOP and I called it the five-point client retention star and it was a pretty simple layout that I want to go through with you here today."   [02:02]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Point #1: ONBOARDING"   NÚMEROS:  Ponto 1 de 5   VISUAL:  Primeiro ponto da estrela destacado, ênfase no processo de onboarding como fundação   [02:05] "To be clear, the first and one of the most critical things when it comes to retention is onboarding. Okay, so through the years I discovered from honestly just fucking up how critical onboarding really is."   [02:18] "So, first of all, when we get a new deal, you might just think onboarding is as simple as, you know, like, okay, I'm going to send them over like a questionnaire with something they've got to fill out, or I'm going to get them access to whatever the product or service is, or, you know, I'm going to shoot them over like some emails with expectations of like what's going to happen and how it's going to work."   [02:38] "And obviously, onboarding specific to every individual product and service that's sold, but through the years, I found some very simple commonalities."   [02:47]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Case Study: Eight Sleep Purchase"   NÚMEROS:  $6,000 USD, $6,652 total   VISUAL:  Exemplo de produto Eight Sleep Pod 4, demonstração de experiência de compra como case study   [02:50] "Okay, and allow me to go through this one by one. So, first of all, I just bought a new mattress and bed system. It was called Eight Sleep. Now, Eight Sleep, I would say, ranked poor on my onboarding five-star client retention system."   [03:05] "Here's what happened. First of all, it was about a $6,000 purchase USD and came with a few things. First of all, it came with this thing they call a Pod 4."   [03:18]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Eight Sleep Pod 4 Technology"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Imagem do produto Pod 4, sheet tecnológico que resfria e aquece a cama, tecnologia de monitoramento de sono   [03:22] "Okay, so for everybody who doesn't know, this is essentially like a sheet that goes on top of your bed. And what it does is it cools and heats the bed. And it has this technology in it they call autopilot."   [03:35] "And autopilot is essentially monitoring you while you sleep. If you're familiar with any of the fitness wearables like Oura Ring, Whoop Band, you know, anything else like it could even be a Fitbit or an Apple Watch, a lot of these provide sleep statistics."   [03:50] "Now, this sheet, believe it or not, also provides the same sleep statistics. So, it tracks, you know, REM sleep, deep sleep, hours of sleep, tracks your HRV, heart rate, breathing rate."   [04:05] "In addition to that, this whole autopilot thing that it has built into it will dynamically adjust the temperatures while you're sleeping in order to ensure that, you know, you're sleeping with as much REM and deep sleep as possible."   [04:20]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Pod 4 Ultra - Anti-Snoring Feature"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Base da cama que se eleva automaticamente, demonstração de ajuste para prevenir ronco   [04:25] "Now, you see like the bed here kind of bends a little bit. That's called the Pod 4 Ultra. And long story short, if you have any kind of snoring that it can recognize, it will lift the bed. Not necessarily this dramatically, but it will like slightly adjust the bed's height to ensure that you stop snoring. It's pretty badass."   [04:45] "Now, to be clear, I bought this because at this second house that I have, it was furnished and it came with a queen bed, and I wanted a king bed."   [05:00]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Eight Sleep Website - Purchase Breakdown"   NÚMEROS:  $6,652 total   VISUAL:  Website da Eight Sleep, localização em Nova York, empresa bem financiada, presença em ads   [05:05] "So, when it came time to buy a new bed, I was like, 'Well, fuck, I mean, I might as well go buy the Eight Sleep. I've heard great things about it.' So, you can see here like their website and to be clear, this is a company that operates out of New York here in the USA. They have a pretty large amount of funding that they've raised and I've heard a lot of great things."   [05:25] "A lot of people in the tech world, they buy this mattress and this company runs a lot of ads as well."   [05:32]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Purchase Details"   NÚMEROS:  $6,652 total cost   VISUAL:  Breakdown completo da compra: Pod 4 Ultra, King Mattress, Leg Kit, Autopilot, Sleep Essentials Bundle   [05:35] "So, simply put, if you go to buy this, let's see how much the exact cost was. So, I bought the Pod 4 Ultra, I bought a king mattress, I bought the leg kit and I also got this autopilot thing that does those dynamic things that I talked about. I did buy this sleep essentials bundle and once again I got this king mattress. So it came out to a total cost of $6,652."   [06:00]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Onboarding Failure #1: Delivery Expectations"   NÚMEROS:  June 13-17 (initial), July 13-17 (revised)   VISUAL:  Timeline de expectativas quebradas, mudança de data de entrega de junho para julho   [06:05] "Now to be clear, this is where they really started fucking up. I get an initial email from them that sets an expectation. And the expectation was really simple. It was initially supposed to get delivered in June. So, I get told, okay, middle of June, it was like the 13th through the 17th was when this thing was supposed to show up."   [06:25] "And I was pretty pumped because I ordered it in early June. I ordered it on like June 9th or June 10th or something like that. So when they said it was supposed to show up on the 13th and 17th, I was like, 'Oh, sick.'"   [06:40] "So one thing that they didn't do is they did not make clear whether it was everything or just some things. Because keep in mind, everything that you just saw, that was a lot of stuff. We have the mattress, we have the sleep bundles kit, we have the sheet, we have the leg kit, and we have that base. Those are all individual things that technically came with it."   [07:00] "Now, they set the expectation just in this rather ambiguous, broad email, you're going to get all this between the 13th and 17th. I respond back because once again, they did not make it clear and I simply asked. I was like, 'Hey, is it everything or is it just some things?'"   [07:18] "Now, long story short, it took them about a day. They responded back and they said, 'Hey, it's going to be everything.' So, again, I was like, 'Okay, sick. This is awesome.'"   [07:30]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Customer Emotional Response"   NÚMEROS:  June 13-17 → July 13-17 (1 month delay)   VISUAL:  Frustração do cliente, expectativas quebradas, mudança inesperada de timeline   [07:35] "I then go on sometime between the 13th and 17th and I got another email and that email changed the delivery date to July 13th through the 17th. Put in the chat how you think I felt."   [07:48] "I just spent $6,652. I'm getting, like I said, I mean, I want a fucking king-size bed. I can go down the street to Mattress Firm, you know, and I took a risk here on this Eight Sleep company and they tell me I'm getting it between June 13th and 17th, like days from the day I order it, and then they tell me it's like going to be a month now."   [08:10] "How you guys think I feel? Put in the chat, you know? Yeah. Scammed, disgusted, crappy."   [08:18]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Onboarding Failure #2: Inadequate Compensation"   NÚMEROS:  $50 Amazon gift card   VISUAL:  Resposta inadequada da empresa, tentativa de compensação insuficiente para problema de $6,652   [08:22] "So, I respond back to that email and I'm like, 'Guys, what the fuck?' Like, you just said it was going to be the 13th through the 17th. What am I supposed to do now? You know what they sent back to me? They said, 'Oh, sorry, sir. Here's a $50 Amazon gift card.'"   [08:38] "How you guys think I felt after that? You think that made me happy or you think that made me more mad? Yes. Ash says insulted, even more pissed. Yes, that's right. I was fucking mad as fuck. I was like, 'Are you kidding me, dude?'"   [08:55] "So, anyway, it is what it is, you know. I wasn't mad at my queen bed or anything. It was just, you know, it's not ideal for a guy who's 6'5 and like damn near 280 pounds, you know? I like my king beds, you know what I mean?"   [09:12]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Onboarding Failure #3: Unclear Shipping Communication"   NÚMEROS:  July 10-12 (shipping notification)   VISUAL:  Email ambíguo de envio, falta de clareza sobre quais itens foram enviados   [09:15] "So around this July 13th through 17th time frame, couple days before that, maybe like the 10th through the 12th, I get an email that says like, 'Hey, your order shipped.' Well, that email, it did not say what shipped. It just said your order shipped."   [09:32] "So, keep in mind once again, I got the leg kit, the base, the mattress, the sheet, this little thing on the left you see here that like cools and heats the bed. I got the essentials kit which comes with all this shit."   [09:45] "So, same thing. I'm like, well, what? At this point, do you guys think I trust this company? Fuck no. So, I hit him back and I'm like, well, what specifically shipped and I didn't even get an email response on that."   [10:00] "So, I was like, 'All right, I guess we'll find out.' You know, and keep in mind, I got two houses. So, during this specific time, like this July 13th through 17th time, I don't know if you guys remember or not, but we had our mastermind for Q3 on like I think it was July 19th and 20th or 20th and 21st, I don't remember."   [10:20] "So now I'm thinking to myself like, dude, I got to be here at this house until like the last fucking day before the mastermind. Like, I don't want to do that, you know? I want to go to the other house. So now they're fucking with my schedule."   [10:35]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Onboarding Failure #4: Incomplete Delivery"   NÚMEROS:  July 18-19 (partial delivery), 2 weeks later (pillows)   VISUAL:  Entrega fragmentada, itens chegando em momentos diferentes, falta do colchão inicialmente   [10:38] "You know, this onboarding process was exceptionally poor for a $6,000 purchase. It was so bad. So then sometime between I think it was the 19th or like the 18th, I get a fucking bunch of boxes. I get a base. I get the base comes in two giant boxes. The little cooler thing comes in a giant box. The sheet comes in an even bigger box. I don't get the mattress."   [11:00] "And I get a bunch of random accessories that came inside of this. By the way, literally yesterday I just got the pillows, by the way, a full two weeks later. So, all over the place in terms of what I got."   [11:15] "And so, anyway, I didn't get the mattress. Keep in mind, I hit them up and I said, 'Does all this shit come together?' And remember, they said, 'Yeah.' So, when they initially said once again, 'Hey, you're going to get all this between June 13th and 17th,' I hit him back up and I said, 'Is all this coming together?' They said, 'Yeah, absolutely, sir.'"   [11:35] "So, okay, great. Well, it did not come all together. So simply put, they failed to meet an expectation that they set."   [11:45]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Onboarding Failure #5: Missing Instructions"   NÚMEROS:  4 hours (assembly time), $6,000 purchase   VISUAL:  Falta de instruções adequadas, QR code requerendo código de ativação não fornecido   [11:48] "I then two days later get the mattress that shows up. And now here's the crazy part. All right, so obviously I'm not going to build the fucking mattress and the whole setup, nor am I going to take apart the old bed. So I just go on Task Rabbit and I hire some dudes to come over here and get all that shit done."   [12:05] "Okay, so some guys show up, you know, they start taking apart the queen bed. They take it out and they start building the king bed. These guys were here for like four hours."   [12:18] "This thing, okay, for $6,000. Do you think it would have come with instructions on how to put it together? So, it had a little card inside of it with a QR code and it said like, 'Hey, scan here for the instructions.' When you scanned here, it said that there was some kind of like activation code that you needed."   [12:38] "There was no way to get the activation code sent to you. They implied that it was already sent in this specific email of your initial order confirmation. So I start looking through my emails. I don't see anything that is some kind of code. Nothing. And it's just like what the fuck you know?"   [12:55]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Onboarding Best Practices - What Should Have Happened"   NÚMEROS:  June 9-10 (order date), July 13-17 (realistic delivery)   VISUAL:  Exemplo de comunicação correta, expectativas claras desde o início, transparência total   [13:00] "It's insane how poor this onboarding experience was. Dramatically poor. Think of all these little things. How simple could this have been? Watch this. Right? I purchased the fucking mattress and the whole shit."   [13:15] "The initial email should have just been, 'Hey, you're gonna get all of this at once. Some things might show up a couple days later. Just FYI, it's all going to show up between July 13th through 17th. And just to be clear, if you have any questions, you know, contact us. We'll respond within this time frame.'"   [13:35] "I wouldn't have had a bad experience if they had just set the expectations correctly the first time around. Right? Think about that. They fucked this whole thing up by telling me, 'Okay, you're going to get it between June 13th and 17th.'"   [13:50] "Yeah, I mean, it's all shipping, you know, it's all going to be there together at the same time. But I had to reach out and ask that. I then get another fucking email that says, 'Okay, nope. It's going to be July 13th through 17th.'"   [14:05] "Now I reach out mad. I'm like, 'What the fuck guys?' And then they're like, 'Oh, here's a $50 gift card.' And then it doesn't all show up together. It shows up randomly days apart. Technically two weeks apart if you count the pillows showing up yesterday." Terrible. Thankfully, the product's good. I will say that, you know, I don't really have any complaints about the actual product itself, which is great. But this thing is fucking nuts with how bad they fucked this up."   [14:42] "So, what's the opposite of that, right? When you look at the five-point client retention star and you just break down the first of the five steps, dude, it all starts with onboarding. And onboarding, in my opinion, is just a fancy word for the expectations being set and met."   [15:00]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Onboarding = Expectations Set & Met"   NÚMEROS:  Ponto 1 de 5   VISUAL:  Definição clara de onboarding como gestão de expectativas, fundação do relacionamento   [15:05] "When you onboard somebody into the offer that you are selling, product, service, doesn't matter. Whatever the expectations are, okay, are what somebody expects to occur. And those initial expectations being met and exceeded are what start the relationship off on a great foot."   [15:25] "How probable do you think I am to recommend Eight Sleep now? I fucking - if somebody ever asked me like, 'Hey, how's the Eight Sleep thing going?' I'd be like, 'Yeah, I mean, look, the product's great. You're going to have a fucking nightmare buying it, setting it up, who knows when the fuck it's going to show up, you know? Like, I mean, good luck.'"   [15:48]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Pre-Selling Strategy Discussion"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Discussão sobre estratégias de pré-venda, comunicação transparente vs ocultação de prazos   [15:52] "I think what they were doing is they were pre-selling, right? Like they were going through a phase where they were trying to get people's money even when they knew they couldn't ship it on time. Like the pre-selling strategy, how do you think companies can do effective pre-selling when they're in that operational state?"   [16:10] "Do you think they just - I mean they if they had told you how long it would have taken, you may have thought twice about buying it."   [16:18] "No, it's right here. That's an interesting question. It's right here. I would not have thought twice about buying it because I already had a mattress. I mean, sure, if they would have said, 'Okay, hey, it's going to take a month from now for you to get your mattress.' Sure, they'd definitely lose some sales. No doubt."   [16:38] "Because somebody out there wants a mattress like now, you know, might be somebody moving in. Like, as an example, you know, this new lease for the penthouse starts on September 1st. It also has a queen-size bed. I am going to remove that bed and put a king-size bed in there."   [16:55] "Knowing what I know now about these guys, if I ordered that bed literally right now, which I probably will, it would take all the way until middle of September for me to actually receive all that most likely towards the tail end of September. And they just set that expectation like that's fine."   [17:15]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Underpromise, Overdeliver Philosophy"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Filosofia clássica de negócios aplicada ao onboarding, gestão conservadora de expectativas   [17:18] "You know, that's the thing. It's like when you're talking about a mattress, especially in this instance, it's just something you got to plan around. So, like I said, it's okay that, you know, it's going to take a little longer than what somebody might want it to as long as they're told that upfront."   [17:35] "If you fail to communicate properly the timeline, once again, and you fail expectations, this is just a very simple thing. The expectations of any business's onboarding process, that's what leads to the initial success of the relationship."   [17:52] "When expectations are met and exceeded, that's interpreted as this is a successful relationship. That's all you got to understand about the onboarding process. It's that simple. And remember, you set the onboarding process. You set the expectations."   [18:10] "You tell people when what is supposed to occur. It's just a bunch of things that you're making up that you need to make sure you can meet and exceed. There's a very simple cliche business quote of underpromise overdeliver."   [18:25]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Marketing Agency Example: $1M/Month Goal"   NÚMEROS:  $1,000,000/month goal, 30-60 days (fastest achievement)   VISUAL:  Exemplo de gestão de expectativas em agência, objetivo vs realidade de timeline   [18:28] "Like in your onboarding process especially, this is where you want to accomplish that. You know, we have in our marketing agency, almost every single client ever that works with us wants to hit a million dollars a month. And we want to hit a million dollars a month with them, too."   [18:45] "Now, to be clear, that's not going to happen in the first month. There's clients that we've worked with where it has happened in the first 30 to 60 days. It has. Now, they hear stories like that, they potentially know those individuals, and then they come talk to us."   [19:02] "We have to brutally downgrade those expectations. Like we have to make sure that we set the bar for like all the scenarios that could occur and we have to lean into the most conservative scenario. We have to tell them like when what is supposed to happen and like I said, we just have to meet and exceed those simple expectations that are set."   [19:25] "It's truly that simple. To be clear, does anybody have questions on the onboarding process? Feel free to unmute yourself and ask away before we continue to the next one."   [19:35]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Onboarding Challenge: Curve Balls & Client Expectations"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Discussão sobre SOPs vs situações inesperadas, clientes tentando redefinir expectativas   [19:38] "Yeah, quick one. So basically on the onboarding process often what happens is that like okay we have our SOPs but there are curve balls that come up and some clients very cheekily try to reset expectations."   [19:55] "For example, very quick example - hair salon in Notting Hill they do really well. He wanted a handover. He said we'll build our landing pages cuz I offered to build them for a fee. He said we'll build them and then he didn't end up doing the timings correctly on his end in terms of the handover between one agency and the other."   [20:15] "And what ended up happening is he was like asking us, pleading with us, can you run our ads for an extra week on both Google and Facebook? Now, I made a meet him halfway concession because I wanted to meet him halfway and show good rapport building."   [20:32] "And he also is the CFO of a massive crypto startup that he said he was, you know, dangling the carrots of future bigger agency deals for me. So, I'm not going to go and break his goodwill and say, no, Mr. Customer, this is what's in the contract. So, I'm interested in your thinking on that."   [20:50] "Yeah, that's how it goes. I mean, that's something that'll come up. I'd consider that one of the other points of the five-point client retention star, which I'll talk about here in a minute. I'd categorize that under the added value category."   [21:05] "So, I basically did the right thing, right, to meet him."   [21:08] "No, you did a great thing. That's not a bad thing at all. I mean, that's a great way to be able to retain deals for sure. Yeah. Well, we'll get to that here in a bit. But that's a good example for sure."   [21:22]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Point #2: RESULTS"   NÚMEROS:  Ponto 2 de 5   VISUAL:  Segundo ponto da estrela destacado, ênfase em resultados como elemento crítico   [21:25] "Okay. To be clear, this is the most important thing in the client retention star in my opinion. But if the other things aren't met as well, it won't matter. You can still lose deals."   [21:38] "I've had clients that we've gotten tremendous results for and we failed to do like two or three of the other client retention star tactics and we'll still lose those relationships."   [21:50] "However, I do want to put a massive emphasis on this one - without results coming in, logically, once again, the client retention star fails."   [22:02]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Eight Sleep Results Example"   NÚMEROS:  65°F (temperature setting), 55°F (autopilot adjustment), 29% (REM sleep increase), 66% (snoring reduction), 29 minutes (total snoring time)   VISUAL:  Exemplo de resultados tangíveis do produto, métricas de sono melhoradas, tecnologia funcionando   [22:05] "Okay, to be clear, let's go back to the Eight Sleep example. So when it comes to these people's product, well, after it was all set up and built, you know, I got really good results with it. I mean, I really enjoy the additional sleep metrics that it provides outside of my Whoop band."   [22:25] "You know, it gives me a way to like measure against my existing device that I use to track all of my important sleep analytics. And I found a lot of value in that."   [22:38] "Interestingly enough, I've historically used this thing called a Chili Pad. For everybody who doesn't know what a Chili Pad is, it's a pretty simple thing. A Chili Pad is essentially just a little sheet that goes on top of your bed and it has the cooling components and the heating components, but it doesn't have all the technology like the tracking technology."   [23:00] "It doesn't have that autopilot feature to like adjust things. It doesn't come with a bed base to like prevent snoring and things like that."   [23:10] "So, long story short, this Eight Sleep upgrade, as soon as I actually slept on it, I mean, dude, it was awesome. I had set the temperature of the bed to about 65 degrees Fahrenheit which is what I set the temperature to in my house as well at night. So I sleep in a very cold environment both in the bed and in the environment itself."   [23:32] "Now to be clear that combo has helped me tremendously with things like my heart rate variability going up dramatically, my resting heart rate while I'm sleeping going down dramatically, my breathing rate going down dramatically. And this is all really good things when it comes to sleep."   [23:50] "It increases my REM sleep and deep sleep and my overall restoration when I wake up the next morning. Now, what was interesting about this first night, this little technology they have in this thing called autopilot, dude, it dropped the temperature at one point to like 55 degrees."   [24:08] "And I remember in the middle of the first night, I was like, 'Holy shit this thing is cold. Like, what the fuck is going on?' And anyway, I woke up the next day and it had told me, it was like, 'Hey, you know, last night we extended your REM sleep by 29% using autopilot.'"   [24:28] "And it like explained how it did it. It was like, you know, we recognized that your body temperature was a little elevated in comparison to where we wanted it at knowing where it should be to get the most REM sleep. So, we dropped the temperature of the bed about 10 degrees. So, we extended to REM sleep 29% by doing that."   [24:50] "In addition to that, I don't think I snore. Like, I don't sit there and like fucking snore all night by any means. I'm usually a side sleeper, so it rarely happens. However, it told me that there was a total of like 29 minutes throughout the entire night where like there was random snoring."   [25:10] "And apparently the bed like lifted up slightly during that window and it claimed that it lowered my snoring by 66%. And I was like, 'What the fuck?' Like, really?"   [25:22] "And over the next - I've had this bed now for about a week and a half and dude it's insane. Like it's actually really really good. I thoroughly enjoy the results that this fucking crazy technology has gotten for me."   [25:38]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Eight Sleep Features: Thermal Alarm & Vibration"   NÚMEROS:  9:00 AM (alarm time), 90°F (wake temperature)   VISUAL:  Recursos adicionais do produto, alarme térmico e vibração como alternativa a alarmes tradicionais   [25:42] "It's also got this thing called a thermal alarm and it has vibration technology in it as well. So rather than waking up to any kind of alarm, which I never do anyway, at 9:00 a.m., god forbid, I'm still sleeping at 9:00 a.m., the temperature of the bed will raise to about 90°."   [26:00] "So the bed will simply put just get hot, which will naturally wake you up. And if you're still not awake by 9:00 a.m., it'll vibrate. And like it's a very comfortable vibration. It happened to me today cuz I went to bed at like 1:00 a.m. last night."   [26:18]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Results Despite Poor Onboarding"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Produto excelente compensando experiência de onboarding ruim, poder dos resultados   [26:22] "Now, my point is this is sick. Like I love this technology. In terms of results, like they accomplished the results, I thoroughly enjoy it. The onboarding process was absolutely fucked but when it comes to the results that it's generated me, I'm a fan."   [26:40] "And this is a lot of what they claimed they could accomplish with this specific technology. Now, to be clear, results, they happen in different forms. There's small wins and then there's like the big. Usually, it's one, but sometimes, to be fair, it's multiple."   [27:00]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Small Wins vs Big Wins"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Estratégia de comunicação de resultados progressivos, pequenas vitórias levando à grande vitória   [27:02] "Usually what happens first is the small wins. This is important to understand. So we just onboarded a new deal about a week and a half ago and the very first results - it was a call funnel. So it was a lead generation client. They have a sales team."   [27:20] "What are the first results that come in on a call funnel? Well, first of all, I mean, just the fact they get approved, you know, like the small initial wins can be something as simple as, hey, the ads just got edited. Here they are if you want to check them out."   [27:38] "Okay, we just put everything into review. Oh, hey, check this out. Everything just got approved. We're officially live. Hey, the first lead just came in, you know. And to be clear, it's also real and it looks qualified."   [27:55] "Like, check this out. Here's the lead's data. Here's their application data and they scheduled a call at this time on this date. Those are an example of small wins."   [28:08]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Client Goal Example"   NÚMEROS:  $1,000,000/month (big win goal), $45 (cost per call)   VISUAL:  Objetivo de grande escala vs resultados incrementais, gestão de expectativas ao longo do tempo   [28:12] "The big win, this particular client that I'm talking about, they want to get to a million a month with paid advertising. It's a big win. It's going to take a lot longer. I can't just not communicate any kind of result."   [28:28] "You know, in between then and now, I have to communicate a bunch of small wins as well. I have to communicate essentially the progress that is happening towards that goal."   [28:40] "So, hey, first lead just came in. Look, they're qualified. Here's their application data. Hey, we're at this many calls now. Our cost per call's this. That's this in comparison to the average from all our other cost per call clients."   [28:55] "And in this particular case, by the way, client's getting about a $45 cost per scheduled qualified call. That's tremendous. That's way below average in comparison to everybody else. So, we'll tell them that, too. We share that data with them."   [29:12] "As soon as we hit certain benchmarks, like, hey, we just got the first five, we just got the first 10, we just got the first 25, we just got the first 50."   [29:22]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Sales Team Feedback Loop"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Comunicação com equipe de vendas, qualidade de leads, show rates, feedback contínuo   [29:25] "Then we'll start doing things like reaching out to the sales staff. We'll try to understand the quality from the sales team's perspective. And if the sales team tells us, hey, yeah, these things are great. You know, we're looking good, we'll let the client know."   [29:42] "If the sales team gives us negative feedback and says, hey, you know, leads aren't coming in that great, like our show rates fucked up. That's still an opportunity to talk about a result."   [29:55] "We're going to reach out to the client. We're going to say, 'Hey, sales team gave some feedback that their show rate's sucking ass. We're going to work with them a little bit to get the show rate up. It's currently this - let's peg that as a benchmark for where they're at.'"   [30:12] "And we're going to send them things like our show rate and connection rate mastery SOP. We're going to potentially do some sales training with those closers if we need to to help them understand what they need to do."   [30:28] "We're going to potentially tweak marketing automation or text automation reminders. We're going to do whatever we can to make sure that we get that up."   [30:38]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Communicating Problems as Opportunities"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Transformar problemas em oportunidades de comunicação, transparência sobre desafios e soluções   [30:42] "Now, as an example, even if something's fucked up, like we'll acknowledge it's fucked up, but then once again, as we're working to fix it and make it better, we're going to acknowledge the things that are happening to make it better until it's eventually better. And then we communicate that."   [31:00] "Results come in small wins and they come in big wins. Just like with Eight Sleep, it's like, dude, the first night was really simple. They hit me with an email as well and they said, hey, how was your first night's sleep on the pod? You know, like give us some survey feedback. And everything was great. I mean, I loved it a lot."   [31:20] "They sent me a text in the morning. Every morning I get a text now from them with my sleep stats. I can open the app and I can see all my sleep stats. I can open the app and see what autopilot did in that particular night to help me like sleep longer and maximize my sleep statistics that I want to have maximized, reduce the ones that are bad."   [31:42] "I mean, dude, those are all small wins. The big wins, and this is pretty interesting when you look at it from the mattress product, the big one would just be long periods of time of consistently better sleep in comparison to how it was before, but you can't get that until a lot of time passes."   [32:00]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Small Wins Create Momentum"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Acúmulo de pequenas vitórias gerando momentum, participação do cliente no processo   [32:05] "So, small wins, they're doing a good job with that. Results are simple. Whatever the client paid you to help them get, you've got to help them with. But remember, usually whatever that is, it takes a longer duration of time."   [32:20] "You fail to retain clients when you only focus on the big wins. You have to focus on the small wins first. And when you stack a bunch of small wins, that feels like momentum."   [32:32] "And when something has momentum to it, there's a higher probability that you get to the big win faster, believe it or not, because the client will start participating in it and helping the win."   [32:45]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Communication Impact on Perception"   NÚMEROS:  10 degrees (temperature drop)   VISUAL:  Exemplo de como comunicação transforma experiência negativa em positiva   [32:50] "Now imagine once again - imagine my first night in the Eight Sleep if I interpreted everything that happened as a negative. Like the fucking temperature dropping 10 degrees and me freezing my ass off in the middle of the night almost waking me up."   [33:08] "Imagine if they didn't communicate with me like the bed lifting up and like reducing snoring or something like that. Imagine if they didn't communicate why the temperature dropped. I would have woken up and been like is this thing fucking broken? Like it dropped 10 degrees below where I set it to. What the fuck was that about?"   [33:28] "You know, but because once again they communicated it and they told me what was going on. Now I'm sitting here and I'm telling you guys as an example. I don't know how many people there are on this call or who will listen to it but moral of the story is I'm telling all you guys, yeah, it's a great product, you know, works great. Onboarding is a little fucked but like the results awesome, you know?"
